summary: Cao Cao; holds the great banquet at the newly-completed Bronze Bird Tower at Yejun. Watches his sons and officers compete in archery for the silk robe; receives the latest news of Jingzhou. Begins the political theater that will lead him to the Duke of Wei rank later in the chapter.
Cao Xiu; among the young Cao kinsmen who compete at the Bronze Bird Tower archery.
Xu Chu; cuts through the contest by snatching the prize robe - Cao Cao laughs and keeps the peace.
Xu Huang; competes at the Bronze Bird Tower archery.
Xiahou Yuan; competes at the Bronze Bird Tower archery.
Wen Pin; serves at the banquet.
Cao Hong; at the banquet.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Zhang He; at the banquet.
Chen Lin; at Cao Cao's court - pens Cao Cao's self-justifying memorial that the chapter excerpts.
Cheng Yu; at Cao Cao's court.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Hua Xin; rising adviser at Cao Cao's court - will be one of the key figures of the next phase.
Wang Lang; at Cao Cao's court.
Wang Lang (duplicate record).
Zhong Yao; at Cao Cao's court.
Wang Can; literary figure at Cao Cao's court.

Zhou Yu; pretends to plot the 'Way to Take Xichuan' that would let his army cross Jingzhou; Zhuge Liang sees through it and welcomes the army at the borders. Zhou Yu collapses again - third great humiliation.

Zhuge Liang; reads Zhou Yu's 'borrow a road to attack Shu' scheme; reroutes Liu Bei's whole defence to greet Zhou Yu's army at the river with cheering troops and a bow. Zhou Yu coughs blood and falls into a swoon at the sight.
Liu Bei; sets up the cheerful welcome that ruins Zhou Yu's pretext.
Zhao Yun; commands part of the welcome ambush.
Huang Zhong; commands part of the welcome ambush.
Wei Yan; commands part of the welcome ambush.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Guan Yu; commands part of the welcome ambush.
Zhang Fei; commands part of the welcome ambush.
Cheng Pu; with Zhou Yu.
Gan Ning; with Zhou Yu.
Huang Gai; with Zhou Yu.
Huang Gai (duplicate record).
Han Dang; with Zhou Yu.
Jiang Qin; with Zhou Yu.
Ding Feng; with Zhou Yu.
Ding Feng (duplicate record).
Xu Sheng; with Zhou Yu.
Sun Quan; receives Zhou Yu's failure.
Lu Su; with Sun Quan.
Gu Yong; at Sun Quan's court.
Zhang Zhao; at Sun Quan's court.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Lady Sun; now at Jingzhou with Liu Bei.
Lady Sun (duplicate record).
Mi Zhu; with Liu Bei.
Dong Zhuo; named in passing.
Liu Biao; named in passing.
Liu Zhang; named in passing in the regional roll.
Yuan Shao; named in passing.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Yuan Shu; named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Zhou Yu dies in this chapter at Baqiu, aged 36. Receives Zhuge Liang's letter that finishes his spirit; his final words 'Heaven, since you made Zhou Yu, why also create Zhuge Liang?' enter the canon. Recommends Lu Su as his successor.
Lu Su; succeeds Zhou Yu as Wu's chief military officer per Zhou Yu's dying recommendation.
Zhuge Liang; travels to Chaisang for Zhou Yu's funeral - delivers the moving lament that wins over even hostile Wu officers. Then recruits Pang Tong on the road home.

Pang Tong; arrives at Liu Bei's court after Zhou Yu's funeral. Initial cold reception when he hides his recommendation letters; sent to manage Leiyang County where he sleeps for 100 days then disposes of all backed-up cases in half a morning. Promoted to senior counsel.
Liu Bei; receives Pang Tong; initially under-rates him, then promotes him to second strategist after the Leiyang demonstration. Wu and Shu now have parallel brain trusts of two.
Zhao Yun; escorts Zhuge Liang to and from Zhou Yu's funeral.
Ma Teng; in the chapter's second half - lured to Xuchang by Cao Cao under pretext of an imperial summons. Sets up his upcoming death in chapter 58.
Ma Chao; with his father Ma Teng - does not accompany him to Xuchang per Ma Teng's worried orders. The setup for his vengeance arc.
Ma Dai; Ma Teng's nephew; stays in Xiliang.
Ma Tie; Ma Teng's second son; rides with Ma Teng to Xuchang.
Ma Xiu; Ma Teng's third son; rides with Ma Teng to Xuchang.
Ma Xiu (duplicate record).
Han Sui; named in passing as Ma Teng's old western ally.
Dong Cheng; named in passing in the chapter's recap of his belt-decree.
Cao Cao; lures Ma Teng to Xuchang.
Xun Yu; in Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Cao Hong; with Cao Cao.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Xiahou Yuan; with Cao Cao.
Xu Chu; with Cao Cao.
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao.
Li Jue; named in passing - long dead.
Guo Si; named in passing - long dead.
Sun Quan; mourns Zhou Yu; receives Lu Su as the new senior commander.
Guan Ping; with Liu Bei.
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Zhang Fei; with Liu Bei.
Sun Zi; named in passing in a literary aside.
Wu Qi; named in passing in a literary aside.
Zhou Xun; minor figure named in passing.
Zhou Yin; minor figure named in passing.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.

Ma Teng dies in this chapter - Cao Cao discovers the renewed conspiracy when Ma Teng arrives at Xuchang; ambushes him with Xu Chu's hidden troops; executes him along with sons Ma Tie and Ma Xiu. The act that triggers Ma Chao's western revolt.
Ma Tie; killed with his father Ma Teng at Xuchang.
Ma Xiu; killed with his father Ma Teng at Xuchang.
Ma Xiu (duplicate record).

Ma Chao; receives news of his father's execution; raises the Xiliang army in revenge with Han Sui. Crushes Cao Cao's first relief column under Zhong Yao; takes Changan; chases Cao Cao across the Yellow River and is within an arrow's range of taking him at the Tong Pass - the chapter's set piece.
Han Sui; joins Ma Chao's vengeance army with his Xiliang troops.
Ma Dai; Ma Chao's cousin; rides at his side.
Pang De's debut - fighting under Ma Chao's banner against Cao Cao at Tong Pass. Will become one of Wei's great generals after Cao Cao reclaims him.
Ma Wan; Ma Chao's clan officer.
Cao Cao; ambushed by Ma Chao at Tong Pass; cuts off his beard and discards his red robe to escape recognition; barely saved by Cao Hong who holds the river bank with his sword.
Cao Hong; holds the river bank with his sword for six days to cover Cao Cao's escape across the Wei - the second great life-saving feat of his career.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; carries Cao Cao on his back to the boats during the escape; takes arrows in his shield.
Zhong Yao; defending the western frontier; defeated by Ma Chao and chased into Changan.
Li Tong (duplicate record).
Li Tong (duplicate record).
Dian Wei; named in passing - long dead.
Cao Ren; ordered to hold Tong Pass at any cost.
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao at Tong Pass.
Zhang Liao; with Cao Cao at Tong Pass.
Zhang He; with Cao Cao at Tong Pass.
Xiahou Yuan; with Cao Cao at Tong Pass.
Yu Jin; with Cao Cao at Tong Pass.
Chen Qun; Wei minister; named in passing.
Chen Qun (duplicate record).
Zhang Han; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Lu Su; with Sun Quan.
Sun Quan; named in passing.
Liu Bei; named in passing at Jingzhou.
Zhuge Liang; named in passing at Jingzhou.
Cheng Yi; minor figure named in passing.
Cheng Yin; minor figure named in passing.
Li Kan; minor figure named in passing.
Liang Xing; minor figure named in passing.
Li Chao; minor figure named in passing.
Hou Xuan; minor figure named in passing.
Cao Yong; minor figure named in passing.
Ding Fei; gives up his beard-trimming horse to Cao Cao in the escape.
Yang Qiu; Han Sui officer.
Zhu Ling; with Cao Cao.

Cao Cao; the famous letter trick that breaks the Ma Chao / Han Sui alliance. Sends Han Sui a letter with the key parts deliberately smudged so Ma Chao will think they were redacted to hide treachery. The alliance fractures; Ma Chao tries to kill Han Sui (cuts off his arm), then is routed in the field by Xu Chu.

Ma Chao; tricked into suspecting Han Sui by the smudged letter; tries to kill him, cuts off Han Sui's arm; then loses the great single combat with Xu Chu (the famous shirtless duel). Forced to flee west to Zhang Lu at Hanzhong.
Han Sui; the smudged letter target; loses his arm to Ma Chao's strike; defects to Cao Cao with the remnant of his Xiliang army.
Xu Chu; the legendary shirtless duel - strips to the waist to fight Ma Chao without armour over more than 230 bouts at Wei Bridge. Cao Cao's defining bodyguard moment of this campaign.
Jia Xu; designs the smudged-letter trick to break the Ma Chao / Han Sui alliance - the chapter's defining stratagem.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Ma Dai; flees west with Ma Chao.
Pang De; flees west with Ma Chao.
Ma Teng; named in passing - already dead.
Ma Wan; killed by Cao Cao's army.
Yang Qiu; killed by Cao Cao's army.
Li Kan; minor figure named in passing.
Liang Xing; killed in the campaign.
Hou Xuan; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Lu; lord of Hanzhong; receives Ma Chao when he flees west - the setup for chapter 65.
Zhang Wei; Zhang Lu's brother; named in the Hanzhong intro.
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Zhang Ling; Zhang Lu's grandfather; named only in his Daoist lineage.
Zhang Heng; Zhang Lu's father; named only in his lineage.
Zhang Heng (duplicate record).
Zhang Jia; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Yan; named in passing.
Liu Zhang; Yi Province lord; warned by his officers about the Cao Cao threat now closing on his northern border. Sets up his appeal to Zhang Lu / Liu Bei.
Liu Yan; Liu Zhang's late father; named in his lineage.
Liu Yan (duplicate record).
Liu Yan (duplicate record).
Liu Yan (duplicate record).
Pang Xi; Liu Zhang's general at Baxi.
Pang Xi (duplicate record).
Lou Gui; Liu Zhang's adviser.
Wei Kang; loyal officer who tries to defend Liang Province against the Ma Chao defectors.
Yang Fu; the avenger of Wei Kang; raises troops against Ma Chao.
Yan Pu; Zhang Lu's officer.
Cao Hong; with Cao Cao.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Ren; with Cao Cao.
Xiahou Yuan; with Cao Cao.
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao.
Yu Jin; with Cao Cao.
Xun You; with Cao Cao.
Zhu Ling; with Cao Cao.

Zhang Song's debut - Liu Zhang's adviser sent to Cao Cao seeking alliance against Zhang Lu. Cao Cao treats him with contempt over his ugly appearance and dismisses him; he memorises Cao Cao's military arrangements, then defects on the road home to Liu Bei in Jingzhou with the maps of Yi Province in his sleeve. The shift that opens Shu to Liu Bei.
Zhang Song (duplicate record).
Cao Cao; insults Zhang Song over his appearance and turns him away - the chapter's single most-cited Cao Cao miscalculation, sending Yi Province directly into Liu Bei's lap.

Yang Xiu; tests Zhang Song with Cao Cao's military treatise; Zhang Song recites it back word for word - proving the treatise is a copy of an older work. The Yang Xiu / Zhang Song exchange is the chapter's standout intelligence scene.
Yang Xiu (duplicate record).
Liu Bei; lavishly entertains Zhang Song on his way home; receives the Yi Province maps and Zhang Song's promise to make him welcome in Liu Zhang's court. The setup for the Western campaign.
Liu Zhang; receives Zhang Song's recommendation to bring Liu Bei into Yi Province as a counter to Zhang Lu - sows the seeds of his own dispossession.
Fa Zheng's debut - Liu Zhang's officer; secretly aligned with Zhang Song's plan to bring in Liu Bei. Will become one of Liu Bei's two senior advisers.
Meng Da; rides with Fa Zheng to invite Liu Bei into Yi Province.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Wang Lei; loyal Liu Zhang officer who protests bringing in Liu Bei; hangs himself from the gate to make the protest heard.
Huang Quan; loyal Liu Zhang officer; opposes inviting Liu Bei; warned and dismissed.
Li Hui; Liu Zhang officer; sides with Liu Bei after the invitation.
Li Hui (duplicate record).
Zhuge Liang; sees the Yi-Province opening and counsels Liu Bei to march.
Pang Tong; with Liu Bei - primary strategist for the upcoming Western campaign.
Guan Yu; assigned to garrison Jingzhou with Zhuge Liang while Liu Bei goes west.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu at Jingzhou.
Zhang Fei; named in passing - will join the Yi expedition in chapter 63.
Zhao Yun; named in passing - will join the Yi expedition.
Huang Zhong; with Liu Bei at Jingzhou.
Wei Yan; with Liu Bei at Jingzhou.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Liu Feng; with Liu Bei.
Liao Hua; with Liu Bei.
Ma Chao; named in passing - now at Hanzhong.
Zhang Lu; named in passing - Liu Zhang's nominal target.
Zhang Ji; named in passing.
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Ji (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu; named in passing.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Mi Heng; named in passing in the chapter's literary recap.
Sun Quan; named in passing.
Zhou Yu; named in passing - long dead.
Xun Yu; in Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Yang Biao; minister in Xuchang.
Sun Zi; named in passing in a literary aside.
Wu Qi; named in passing in a literary aside.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
